summary: Policy-backed control for the Store option in the Open With dialog. Good for managed or offline-first systems, but it removes a fallback path for finding an app when a file type is unknown.

setting.name: Store in Open With
setting.casual_explanation: When Windows cannot find an app for a file type, it can offer a Store-based option in the Open With dialog. This policy decides whether that Store option is available.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it on managed, offline, or privacy-focused systems where they do not want Windows suggesting the Store as a fallback.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off makes the Open With dialog simpler and more local-only, but it removes one of the built-in ways casual users discover an app for an unknown file type.
setting.professional_notes: This setting maps directly to NoUseStoreOpenWith under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer. ICM.admx uses enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the Store option off and standard behavior applies.

allowed_values.label: Store option allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The Store service can still appear in Open With.

allowed_values.label: Store option blocked
allowed_values.meaning: The Store service is not offered in Open With for unknown file associations.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoUseStoreOpenWith = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default

states.rationale: Policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: A missing value is the cleanest representation of the Windows policy baseline for this setting.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want Open With to keep its usual fallback behavior

states.rationale: Leave Windows at its standard policy baseline.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the normal Open With experience for unknown file types.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to suppress Store-based suggestions.
tradeoffs: Windows can still suggest the Store.

recommended_profiles.label: Local only Open With
intended_for: Users who do not want Store suggestions
avoid_for: Casual users who use the Store fallback

states.rationale: Hide the Store option from Open With.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a more local-only and policy-controlled file association experience.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to help discover apps through the Store.
tradeoffs: The Store option disappears from Open With.
Unknown file type discovery becomes more manual.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_ICM Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that controls whether the Store service is offered in Open With.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ICM.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows NoUseStoreOpenWith with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoUseStoreOpenWith = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact Open With Store policy surface, value semantics, and dialog behavior. The app writes the documented disable value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="ShellNoUseStoreOpenWith_2" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer" valueName="NoUseStoreOpenWith">; ICM.adml: If you enable this policy setting, the "Look for an app in the Store" item in the Open With dialog is removed.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact machine-policy key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy removes the Store entry from the Open With dialog.
